message:
Add structure-aware AOF/RDB round-trip proptests and close frankenredis-hjnz

Land the implementation side of frankenredis-hjnz ("Add structure-
aware AOF/RDB round-trip property tests to fr-persist"), and flip the
beads ticket from in_progress to closed with a close_reason that
points at this commit — the implementation and the ledger update
travel together so `br ready` and `bv --robot-triage` never see a
state where the tests are on disk but the ticket still reads open.

Coverage extensions inside `crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs`:

Strategies added in the existing `mod fuzz` sub-module so they reuse
the same `proptest::prelude::*` import and sibling helpers:

- `byte_vec_strategy(max_len)` — arbitrary byte slices up to
  `max_len`, used for both AOF argv tokens and short binary fields.
- `non_empty_byte_vec_strategy(max_len)` — same but length 1..=max.
- `finite_score_strategy()` — bounded-domain f64 generator that
  yields deterministic finite scores plus explicit +0.0 / -0.0, so
  stream-entry generators never drag NaN/inf through the codec path
  and shadow real regressions.
- `aof_record_strategy()` — produces `AofRecord { argv }` with 1..=6
  argv entries, each up to 16 bytes.
- `stream_entry_strategy()` — produces `crate::StreamEntry` triples
  (ms, seq, pairs-of-bytes) with bounded cardinality for determinism.
- `rdb_entry_strategy()` — produces 0..=8 `RdbEntry` records keyed
  on (db, key, value, expire_ms), using a BTreeMap feeder so the
  shrink order stays stable.
- `aux_fields_strategy()` — auxiliary field sets drawn from
  `string_regex("[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,7}")` keys and
  `string_regex("[A-Za-z0-9._:-]{0,12}")` values, up to 4 entries.

Two new proptest blocks register the round-trip properties against
the existing `encode_aof_stream` / `decode_aof_stream` and
`encode_rdb` / `decode_rdb` public functions:

- `encode_decode_aof_stream_round_trips` — for any 0..=8-element
  vector of generated AOF records, `decode_aof_stream(
  encode_aof_stream(rec))?` must equal the input vector. Wrapped in
  `ProptestConfig::with_cases(256)` so it stays cheap in CI but
  still beats a hand-written fixture list.
- `encode_decode_rdb_round_trips` — for any generated (entries,
  aux_fields), re-encode via `encode_rdb(&entries, &aux_refs)` and
  then `decode_rdb(&encoded)` and assert both the entries vec and
  the `BTreeMap<String, String>` aux map come back identical. Aux
  fields are promoted from owned `(String, String)` pairs to the
  `(&str, &str)` form `encode_rdb` expects via a local refs vector.

Two small unrelated import-cleanup touches land in the same file to
keep rustfmt happy after the additions:

- `super::{...}` imports for the AOF and RDB tests are re-ordered to
  alphabetical (cosmetic; no symbol additions or removals).
- `proptest::string::string_regex` and `std::collections::BTreeMap`
  are brought into the `fuzz` sub-module so the new strategies compile.

Beads transition:

- frankenredis-hjnz — status in_progress -> closed, closed_at
  2026-04-16T21:49:56Z, close_reason "Added structure-aware AOF/RDB
  round-trip property tests in fr-persist and verified with rch.".
  Priority and dependencies are unchanged.

Both files move in a single commit because the JSONL update is only
honest if the code it points at is present in the same tree; splitting
would leave a transient state where the ledger claims completion of
tests that don't exist yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
